findings.message: The new continuation line is dedented out of the `description: |` block, so this frontmatter is no longer valid YAML and the `rollback-diagnoser` skill wrapper can fail to load. Keep `external PR-reviewer comments need` indented with the rest of the block.

D2's `impl-plan` scope preserves `impl-plan.json` and `task-coverage.json` but omits `task-contract.json`. In this repository `task-contract.json` is an accepted Phase 3 artifact authored by `impl-planner` and consumed by the pre-review `task-contract` gate; the current `plan-artifacts` glob covers it via `track/items/<track-id>/**`. With the proposed split it falls into implicit `other`, leaving a Phase 3 SoT/pre-review contract artifact without a semantic review scope. Add it to `impl-plan` or explicitly classify it as operational if it is intentionally excluded.

`grep -r 'plan-artifacts' .` will also match this track's own plan artifacts (`plan.md`, `impl-plan.json`, `spec.json`, etc.), which still contain many planned uses of the term and are not scheduled for rewrite. As written, T005 cannot ever satisfy AC-13 unless the search scope excludes the track artifact directory or these files are rewritten too.

`task-coverage.json` only maps `IN-*` / `OS-*` / `CN-*` / `AC-*` entries to tasks; the three `GO-*` spec elements in `spec.md` are never assigned anywhere. That leaves 3 of the 28 stated spec elements without task coverage, so the plan's full-coverage claim is not actually represented by the artifact set.

`AC-10` requires removing `plan-artifacts` from both the `Mission` and `Trigger inputs` sections of `.harness/capabilities/rollback-diagnoser.md`, but `T003` only schedules edits for `Trigger inputs` plus the other trigger-description mirrors. If the `Mission` section still contains the old scope name, this plan leaves a required acceptance criterion unmet.

T003 narrows the rollback-diagnoser migration to `impl-plan scope` everywhere, but the spec’s stated goal is to replace the old `plan-artifacts` review surface with four SoT scopes (`adr` / `spec` / `types` / `impl-plan`) while keeping rollback-diagnoser aligned with those boundaries. Since the old `plan-artifacts findings` trigger covered findings from all upstream plan artifacts, changing it only to `impl-plan` drops `adr`, `spec`, and `types` review findings from the documented diagnoser trigger path. Update the task/AC wording to cover the relevant SoT review scopes, or explicitly justify why only impl-plan findings should be diagnosable.

T005's verification is scoped too narrowly to satisfy CN-04's requirement that no live `plan-artifacts` scope-name references remain. The plan only greps T001-T004's listed change surface, but current non-historical docs outside that list still use the old scope name, for example `.claude/skills/diagnose/SKILL.md` refers to `/track:review plan-artifacts findings` and `knowledge/conventions/enforce-by-mechanism.md` says ADR changes are reviewed by the `plan-artifacts` scope. Since those files are not included in T003/T005, the implementation can complete while leaving stale operational guidance that points reviewers at the deleted scope.

T001 only budgets the `.harness/config/review-scope.json` rewrite plus `review_operational` additions, but `observations.md:38` says the batch also had to add `.claude/skills/**` to the `harness-policy` patterns so T003's `.claude/skills/diagnose/SKILL.md` edit is inside scope. That prerequisite boundary update is not described anywhere in the plan, so T003 is not fully executable from this impl-plan as written.

`observations.md` records AC-13 as passed from `grep -rn 'plan-artifacts\|plan_artifacts' . ...`, but that command scans the whole worktree rather than the T005-scoped surface and is not reproducible with the listed residuals. In the current tree it also matches generated/worktree paths outside the three exclusions documented here, so the recorded “残存ヒットは以下のみ” / AC-13 pass evidence is incomplete. Since T005’s acceptance depends on recording the verification command and result, this observations step has not carried the AC-13 recording mandate through to completion.

`observations.md` records AC-13 as already verified and passing after “T001-T004” were applied, but the executable plan still has only T001 marked `in_progress` and leaves T002-T005 as `todo`; T005 itself says its prerequisite is that T001-T004 are complete. This makes the current impl-plan state incoherent: a future executor following the plan sees T005 evidence already written before the task can legally run, so the AC-13 observation is not tied to the declared task ordering.
